ogTitle: McDougalls Hill vs McGraths Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McDougalls Hill and McGraths Hill.
heading: McDougalls Hill vs McGraths Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McDougalls Hill, New South Wales and McGraths Hill, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McDougalls Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to McGraths Hill (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McDougalls Hill or McGraths Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McDougalls Hill has a flood risk score of 39/100, while McGraths Hill scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McDougalls Hill scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. McGraths Hill scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McDougalls Hill or McGraths Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McDougalls Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
